steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence makes a general claim: Radiohead named themselves in tribute to another band. The second sentence narrows that claim to a specific detail: the exact song (“Radio Head”) and the exact band (Talking Heads). The transition must signal movement from a general statement to a precise elaboration of it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Last of all,: signals the final item in a sequence — there is no sequence here. ✗
  • Choice B — Regardless,: signals dismissal or contrast with something just said — the second sentence doesn’t dismiss the first. ✗
  • Choice C — Instead,: signals contradiction or substitution — the second sentence doesn’t contradict the first. ✗
  • Choice D — Specifically,: signals that precise details follow a general statement, which is exactly what happens here — sentence two names the exact song and band behind the tribute. ✓
That is the correct answer.
